summary: Synthetic candidate review result for deterministic structured-result regression checks.

findings.title: Escalation wording overstates certainty
findings.summary: The synthetic summary now states blocker certainty without evidence caveats and turns the caution into a must-stop instruction.
findings.recommendation: Reintroduce caveated wording and require caller-owned confirmation before any stop decision.

findings.title: Action boundary is explicit
findings.summary: The review result reports concerns without deciding approval, publication, routing, or remediation ownership.
findings.recommendation: Keep downstream policy and action mapping in the embedding repo.

findings.title: Private source label leaked into summary
findings.summary: The synthetic summary names an internal-only source label instead of keeping the evidence reference generic.
findings.recommendation: Replace source-specific wording with a sanitized evidence label before publishing the structured result.

provenance.label: Privacy boundary
provenance.value: No real source paths, tracker identifiers, account data, private facts, or domain policy

markdown:
# Summary
Synthetic candidate review result for deterministic structured-result regression checks.

## Findings
- Critical: Escalation wording overstates certainty.
- Low: Action boundary is explicit.
- High: Private source label leaked into summary.

## Boundary
This fixture uses only generic labels and leaves approval, routing, remediation, and publication policy caller-owned.
